stonetop.summary: Cannibal 'pale demons' of the Labyrinth — corrupted disciples of the Things Below.

system.concept: Cannibal 'pale demons' of the Labyrinth — corrupted disciples of the Things Below.
system.description: Their hair is white, their skin pallid and painted with lime, mud, and blood; their pupils wide like gaping holes; their teeth filed sharp; their weapons obsidian, stone, and bone — and they tan human flesh for leather. The folk of Gordin's Delve know them as the 'pale demons.' They descend from Barrow Builder warlords and sorcerer-kings who fled into the Labyrinth, already depraved disciples of the Things Below; after generations of inbreeding, cave-dwelling, and cannibalism, they're barely human. And yet they keep a language, make deals with outsiders, hate rival tribes more than surface-dwellers, and forage or even cultivate most of their food.

questions.prompt: What do you think the thraulgwyn, the pale demons, really are?
